explicit_text: On the morning of September 11, 2001, I was at Arlington House, preparing to open at 9:30. I was scheduled to be on the second floor, and shortly after arriving, I heard about the World Trade Center being hit. Later, I felt the boom from the plane hitting the Pentagon and saw smoke from the front of Arlington House. We had to close the house and conduct a security sweep, which was nerve-wracking given the uncertainty of the situation.

We had to close the site and conduct a security sweep, which was nerve-wracking given the uncertainty of the situation.

explicit_text: I first heard about the World Trade Center being hit around 9:25 a.m. on September 11, 2001, when Jan, our gardener, rushed in with the news. At first, I thought it was a horrible accident, not realizing there were two towers or the scale of the attack. Later, as we watched the news on a small TV, we saw repeated footage of the planes hitting the towers, which was chilling and surreal.

Later, as we watched the news on a small TV, we saw repeated footage of the planes hitting the skyscrapers, which was chilling and surreal.

explicit_text: On September 11, 2001, while at Arlington House, I heard and felt a big boom which I later learned was the plane hitting the Pentagon. We saw smoke from the direction of the Pentagon, and it became clear that something serious was happening. The proximity of the Pentagon to Arlington made the event feel very immediate and personal, and it was a source of concern for all of us that day.

explicit_text: Linda Kacmarcik was with us at Arlington House on the morning of September 11, 2001. She called me down from the second floor to discuss whether to open the museum building, showing her involvement in operations. Despite the chaos, Linda remained calm and composed, which contrasted with others like Karen who were visibly upset. Linda's steady demeanor helped us manage the situation as best we could.

explicit_text: Frank Cucurullo was the supervisor who arrived at Arlington House shortly after the attacks on September 11, 2001. He organized a security sweep of the house with several of us, including myself, Linda, and Malcolm. Frank was trying to figure out how to respond to unprecedented events, considering the possibility that our site could be a target. He also coordinated who would stay behind while others went home.

explicit_text: I grew up in Chantilly, Virginia, and on September 11, 2001, after leaving Arlington House, I returned to my family home there. It was about thirty miles outside the city, and I ended up hosting friends who couldn’t get home due to disrupted transportation. My mom made us dinner, and we spent the afternoon watching the news, which was a small comfort amid the chaos.

explicit_text: I experienced the September 11 attacks firsthand while working at Arlington House. The day started with news of the World Trade Center being hit, followed by the Pentagon attack, which I felt as a boom. We conducted security sweeps, helped friends stranded by transportation shutdowns, and watched the tragic events unfold on TV. The entire day was surreal, filled with fear, confusion, and a strong sense of community as we supported each other.
